description:
The system's default desktop environment, GNOME3, will mount devices and removable media (such as DVDs, CDs and USB flash drives) whenever they are inserted into the system. To disable automount within GNOME3, add or set automount to false in /etc/dconf/db/distro.d/00-security-settings. For example: 
[org/gnome/desktop/media-handling]
automount=false
Once the settings have been added, add a lock to /etc/dconf/db/distro.d/locks/00-security-settings-lock to prevent user modification. For example: 
/org/gnome/desktop/media-handling/automount
After the settings have been set, run dconf update.
rationale:
Disabling automatic mounting in GNOME3 can prevent
the introduction of malware via removable media.
It will, however, also prevent desktop users from legitimate use
of removable media.
ocil:
These settings can be verified by running the following: 
$ gsettings get org.gnome.desktop.media-handling automount
If properly configured, the output for automount should be false. To ensure that users cannot enable automount in GNOME3, run the following: 
$ grep 'automount' /etc/dconf/db/distro.d/locks/*
If properly configured, the output for automount should be /org/gnome/desktop/media-handling/automount
title: Disable GNOME3 Automounting
